info.title: probectl control-plane API
info.description: Operational endpoints plus the versioned /v1 resource API. All /v1 routes are tenant-scoped; in S9 the tenant is resolved by a dev stub (default tenant, optional X-Probectl-Tenant override) — real identity (SSO/SCIM, per-tenant IdP) lands in S18.

tags.name: agents
tags.description: Registered agents (created via mTLS registration; managed here).

tags.name: changes
tags.description: Change intelligence: the change timeline + change-to-incident correlation (S29).

tags.name: abac
tags.description: Attribute-based access control policies over RBAC (S31).

get.description: Returns 200 when dependencies (the database) are reachable, otherwise 503.

get.summary: Latest discovered network path for a test (path-viz data)

post.summary: Run a path discovery for a test and return the path

get.summary: Get an incident with its signal timeline

200.description: The incident and its time-ordered signals.

get.summary: Read a page of the tenant's tamper-evident audit trail

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return events with seq greater than this cursor.

200.description: A page of audit events plus the next cursor.

get.summary: Verify the integrity of the tenant's audit chain

200.description: The verification result; ok=false with a detail when the chain is broken.

get.summary: The authenticated caller's tenant, identity, and effective permissions

post.summary: Revoke the current session and clear the session cookie

200.description: A cited root-cause answer scoped to the caller's tenant and permissions.

503.description: The AI assistant is temporarily unavailable.

post.summary: Submit feedback (thumbs up/down) on an AI answer

200.description: A schema-valid test proposal — NOT created; apply via POST /v1/tests.

422.description: Could not author a valid config from the request.

post.summary: Propose monitorable targets mined from observed telemetry (ranked, deduped; pending confirmation)

200.description: Ranked discovery proposals — none are created.

parameters.description: Grouping: src | dst | pair | src_asn | dst_asn (default src).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Look-back window as a Go duration (default 1h).

parameters.name: direction

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Look-back window as a Go duration (default 1h).

parameters.description: Bucket size as a Go duration (default window/20, min 1m).

get.summary: Interfaces whose latest bucket departs from their own baseline

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Baseline window as a Go duration (default 1h).

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description: Bucket size as a Go duration (default window/20, min 1m).

parameters.name: min_bps
parameters.description: Ignore interfaces below this current bps (default 1000).

parameters.name: time
parameters.description: Evaluation time (unix seconds or RFC 3339; default now).

post.description: Accepts a Prometheus remote-write v1 WriteRequest. The payload is untrusted: size-, series-, sample-, and label-capped, and every sample's tenant_id label is overwritten with the authenticated caller's tenant.

get.summary: Look up CMDB CIs by IP address or hostname

200.description: Look up CMDB CIs by IP address or hostname

get.description: Read-only CI lookup against the configured CMDB provider (ServiceNow). 503 when no provider is configured.

Per-backend operational health of the S41 secrets resolver: configured schemes (env/vault/cyberark/aws/azure/gcp), resolve/failure counters, live (unexpired) lease counts, last success time, and the last REDACTED error. Never contains secret material or reference fragments. resolver_running=false distinguishes an unwired resolver from a healthy-but-idle one. Requires directory read (admin surface).

The deployment's edition truth: licensed tier (community/enterprise/provider), lifecycle state (community, active, grace, read_only), customer + expiry + read-only horizon when licensed, the provider tenant band, and the full gated-feature table with per-feature licensed/mode status. This endpoint is the ONE place tiers appear when unlicensed (the hidden-unlicensed UX): commercial features are otherwise invisible without an entitlement. License verification is offline (Ed25519 against build-time-baked keys) — never phone-home; expiry degrades read-only after a 30-day grace and never breaks running telemetry.

The caller's tenant's dependency graph (agents, hops, hosts, services, prefixes, ASes, devices) in a layout-agnostic node/edge shape, live or AS IT WAS at ?at= (the versioned-graph contract). Includes the per-plane coverage block (path/flow/routing/device edge counts + honesty notes) so the operator can see what the graph actually contains. topology_running=false distinguishes an unwired store.

post.description: Simulates the failure of one node (e.g. hop:10.0.0.1, service:api, as:64500) or edge (from|kind|to) at a point in time and returns the predicted impact: broken vs rerouted agent→target paths (with the surviving route), transitively impacted services, impacted prefixes, newly disconnected nodes, and SLO impact when the S45 engine is wired. READ-ONLY: the simulation runs on a copy; probectl never acts on predictions (S-EE5 is human-gated). The coverage block reports completeness honestly. Unknown targets are a 404, never an empty 'no impact'.

target.description: Node id or edge id (from|kind|to) to fail.

get.description: The caller's tenant's network-cost picture: egress volume x public pricing attributed per service and per team (showback), traffic-class breakdown (same-zone / inter-AZ / inter-region / internet), cross-AZ 'chatty' zone-pair conversations, hourly trend, and budget status. Honesty flags: cost_running=false (engine unwired), priced=false (volume-only — no price table), zones_mapped=false (no CIDR→zone rules). Pricing provenance (source + as-of date) is surfaced; rates are PUBLIC list ballparks the operator overrides — this is attribution, not billing reconciliation.

The caller's tenant's ESTIMATED network transmission energy and carbon: flow volume × published energy-per-byte coefficients × the operator-set grid intensity, attributed to services/teams through the same mapping as the FinOps engine. The methodology block (measured=false, coefficient source, grid intensity, the estimate note) rides every response — these are coefficient-based estimates for relative attribution and trends, not measured device power. carbon_running=false distinguishes an unwired engine.

The caller's tenant's SLO statuses from the OpenSLO-defined SLIs: attainment over the SLO window, error budget remaining, total events, cold_start honesty flag, and the multi-window burn rates (fast 1h/5m @14.4x, medium 6h/30m @6x, slow 3d/6h @1x — the Google SRE multi-window multi-burn-rate method) with per-window firing state. slo_running=false distinguishes an unwired engine.

The loaded SLO definitions as an OpenSLO v1 YAML stream (multi-document). Lossless: documents round-trip unchanged between probectl and other OpenSLO tooling. Definitions are deployment-level configuration (PROBECTL_SLO_DIR); statuses are tenant-scoped.

The collective internet-outage view for the caller's tenant: shared public outage events (IODA / Cloudflare Radar — opt-in feeds, ingested once) annotated with THIS tenant's affected tests, the tenant's own vantage-detected outages, per-feed health + AUP provenance, and coverage notes. Honesty contract: coverage = your vantage points + public open data — probectl does not operate a global probe fleet; scope_resolution=false reports that IP enrichment is off and correlation is degraded; outage_running=false distinguishes an unwired engine.

The caller's tenant's real-user-monitoring convergence view: per (app, host) window aggregates (views, error rate, p75 LCP/TTFB, per-page-group stats) joined with synthetic coverage into a verdict — healthy, user_impact_confirmed (both planes degraded), synthetic_only_no_user_impact, or user_only_synthetic_blind (the coverage blind spot). The privacy block reports the enforced posture (consent required, URL redaction, no IP storage) and per-reason rejection counters. Claims cover what probectl OBSERVED; absence of RUM data is not proof of health. rum_running=false distinguishes an unwired engine.

post.description: The browser-beacon ingest. NOT session-authenticated: each beacon authenticates itself via its app key (in the body — sendBeacon cannot set headers) and is bound to the KEY's configured tenant, never the payload's. The privacy contract is enforced here, fail-closed: consent must be explicitly true; unknown fields reject the beacon (identifier-bearing payloads can never ingest); URLs are redacted server-side (query/fragment stripped, volatile segments collapsed); the client IP and user agent are never stored. Per-key rate limiting (429 + Retry-After) and a 16KiB size cap (413) bound the surface. CORS: wildcard origin, write-only, credential-less; OPTIONS serves the preflight.

key.description: app key → (tenant, app); an identifier, not a secret; never stored

host.description: bare page host — the synthetic↔RUM join key

get.description: The caller's tenant's per-rule segmentation verdicts from OBSERVED flow/eBPF traffic: violation (forbidden traffic observed, with bounded flow evidence), observed_clean (traffic between the zones observed, none in the forbidden scope), or not_observed (no traffic seen — explicitly NOT proof of isolation). The coverage block states what was actually watched; probectl never claims 'compliant' beyond observed traffic, and never enforces.

A self-verifying, timestamped evidence document: per-rule results with PCI DSS / NIST / zero-trust framework mappings and bounded violation samples, hash-chained via the internal crypto provider (any post-export tampering breaks verification), with the coverage caveats embedded — an auditor sees exactly what was and wasn't observed. Requires audit read.

get.summary: CMDB CIs correlated to an incident's targets

200.description: CMDB CIs correlated to an incident's targets

get.description: Correlates the incident's target and signal targets (tenant-scoped) to CIs in the configured CMDB.

get.summary: CMDB CI correlated to an agent (by hostname)

200.description: CMDB CI correlated to an agent (by hostname)

get.summary: Firing alerts (engine truth) with silence/ack state
get.description: Every series the caller's tenant's alert evaluator currently has firing, with operator state (silenced_until, acked_by/at). evaluator_running=false distinguishes a quiet tenant from one with no running evaluator.

post.summary: Silence a firing alert's notifications until a deadline
post.description: Suppresses channel notifications (and the incident sink) for one firing series for duration_minutes (0 clears, max 7 days). The series keeps evaluating and stays listed as firing; the silence clears automatically on resolve.

404.description: No firing alert with that fingerprint.

503.description: The alert evaluator is not running for this tenant.

post.summary: Acknowledge a firing alert (records who owns it)
post.description: Records the caller as having seen/owning the firing series. Evaluation and delivery are unchanged; the ack clears on resolve.

404.description: No firing alert with that fingerprint.

503.description: The alert evaluator is not running for this tenant.

get.summary: TLS/certificate posture inventory (latest analyzed posture per target)
get.description: The caller's tenant's certificate inventory from the S27 posture pipeline: per-target leaf certificate, protocol/cipher, weakness/CT/threat-intel findings, severity, and the verbatim trustctl handoff payload. collector_running=false distinguishes an unwired collector from an empty fleet.

get.summary: Recent IOC/NDR detections with source attribution + confidence
get.description: The caller's tenant's recent threat detections (newest first): the flagged entity, the matched indicator, the attributing feed/detector with confidence and license, and the correlated incident id (the triage pivot). Detections are confidence-scored SIGNALS — probectl never blocks — and feeds can list benign infrastructure, so provenance ships verbatim. detections_running=false distinguishes unwired consumers from a quiet tenant.

get.summary: Endpoint DEM fleet: WiFi/gateway/last-mile health + slowdown attribution
get.description: The caller's tenant's endpoint fleet (S37 DEM): per-endpoint latest WiFi link, gateway/local-network, last-mile segments, browser sessions, and the attribution verdict (wifi/local/isp/network/none) with confidence — impaired endpoints first. Privacy-minimized fields the agent withheld are absent, never re-derived. collector_running=false distinguishes an unwired consumer from an empty fleet.

get.summary: Latest synthetic result per (type, target, agent) with full detail
get.description: The caller's tenant's newest result per synthetic series, carrying the full per-type detail (DNS rcode/answers/DNSSEC, HTTP dns/connect/tls/ttfb waterfall phases, ICMP/TCP/UDP latency families + loss) — the read model behind the test-result views (S-FE5). collector_running=false distinguishes an unwired consumer from no results yet.

get.summary: Download the tenant's portability bundle (tar.gz: manifest + postgres/<table>.jsonl + flows.jsonl; S-T5 — export is a compliance right, core)
The tenant's retention policy + residency/isolation visibility

put.summary: Set the tenant's flow-retention days (null = deployment default; audited)

post.summary: IRREVERSIBLE verifiable erasure across every store; requires confirm=<tenant slug>; returns the deletion attestation (also appended to the provider audit chain)

TenantHeader.name: X-Probectl-Tenant
TenantHeader.description: Dev stub (S9): a tenant UUID to scope the request to. Omitted → the default tenant. Replaced by session-resolved identity in S18.

Error.description: The standard error envelope returned by every endpoint.

TestRequest.description: The mutable fields of a synthetic test.

target.description: Required for all types except noop.

HopNode.description: One responder at a TTL; multiple nodes at a TTL are ECMP branches.

secret.description: Webhook HMAC secret; redacted (***) in responses.

Signal.description: One plane's observation on an incident timeline (extensible: plane/kind are free-form, attributes is arbitrary).

Me.description: The authenticated caller: the resolved tenant (always present), identity, and the effective RBAC permission keys within that tenant.

AuditEvent.description: One record in the tenant's hash-chained, tamper-evident audit log.

actor.description: Who performed the action (email or user id).

target.description: The id of the affected resource.

next.description: Cursor to pass as ?after= for the next page (0 when no more).

detail.description: Present when ok=false: where and how the chain is broken.

question.description: Natural-language question, e.g. "why is api.example.com slow?"

subject.description: Optional explicit subject pins (target, prefix, node).

prompt.description: e.g. "check Salesforce login from every branch"

ChangeEvent.description: A normalized change event ingested from a signed webhook (S29).

ChangeCandidate.description: A change scored as a candidate cause of an incident.

ABACPolicy.description: An ABAC policy evaluated after RBAC (deny-override).

FlowTopRow.description: One top-talkers row. key is the address/ASN per the grouping; detail carries the pair destination or AS organization name.
